title: Wiring the Terminal Tool
concept: Writing the execution wrapper that runs commands, caps output, and catches exceptions.

exercises.prompt: What does parsing a tool call command help us identify?

options.text: The actions needed to call the tool

exercises.explain: Parsing tool call commands lets us find the actions necessary to call the tool.

cards.title: Wiring the Terminal Tool
cards.body: In this lesson, we will learn how to create an execution wrapper. This wrapper runs commands, limits the output, and handles errors.

cards.title: Execution Wrapper
cards.body: An execution wrapper is a piece of code that runs commands safely and effectively, managing what happens if something goes wrong.

cards.title: Powering a Device
cards.body: Think of the execution wrapper like a power strip. It safely turns on devices, controls the flow of electricity, and prevents overloads!

cards.title: Example of an Execution Wrapper
cards.body: An example wrapper might run a command to list files but only shows the first ten results, substituting an error message if it fails.

cards.title: Myth: All commands run perfectly
cards.body: Many new programmers think every command will work the first time, but errors often happen. This is why we need a wrapper.

cards.title: Execution Process
cards.body: The execution wrapper runs commands, limits outputs, and catches exceptions. Here's how it looks:

exercises.prompt: What is the main purpose of an execution wrapper?

options.text: To safely run commands and handle errors

exercises.explain: The execution wrapper's main purpose is to run commands safely while managing errors.

exercises.prompt: True or False: An execution wrapper can catch exceptions from commands that fail.
exercises.explain: It's true! An execution wrapper is specifically designed to handle exceptions and errors.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the blank: An execution wrapper helps to ______ commands and catch exceptions.
exercises.sentence: An execution wrapper helps to run commands and catch exceptions.
exercises.explain: The execution wrapper is meant to run commands securely and manage any errors that occur.

exercises.prompt: Which of the following describes the job of an execution wrapper?

options.text: Run commands safely and manage errors

exercises.explain: An execution wrapper's job is to safely run commands while managing any errors that might happen.

exercises.prompt: What should an ideal execution wrapper do?

options.text: Run commands, cap output, and handle errors

exercises.explain: An effective execution wrapper should run commands, limit the amount of output it provides, and deal with errors to ensure the program runs smoothly.
